page: Amazon Web Services c7i.24xlarge is a compute-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 8488C processor with an x86_64 architecture, providing 48 physical cores and 96 vCPUs running at a base clock speed of 3.2 GHz. The processor includes 105 MB of L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as Intel Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for matrix operations. The instance is equipped with 192.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, yielding a memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core processing capabilities, with the instance achieving top-tier performance in PassMark CPU and encryption tests, as well as Geekbench image processing. Multi-core stress-ng and Geekbench multi-core scores are categorized as strong, reflecting the efficiency of the 96 dedicated vCPUs. Memory performance is supported by DDR5 technology, showing strong read-only bandwidth for both small cached and large uncached blocks, though large block write bandwidth remains in the average tier. Cryptographic and vector-heavy workloads benefit from hardware-accelerated instructions, as evidenced by strong scores in PassMark extended instructions and high throughput in Redis SET operations.

For networking, the instance provides a baseline bandwidth of 38 Gbps. Because it lacks local NVMe storage and dedicated GPUs, workloads requiring high-speed local scratch space or hardware-accelerated graphics must rely on network-attached storage or alternative instance families. This resource profile offers high qualitative cost efficiency for compute-heavy, CPU-bound tasks where memory density is secondary. It is suited for workloads such as software compilation, image processing, static web serving, database operations, and CPU-based LLM inference, particularly prompt processing where it demonstrates strong benchmarked token throughput.
description: Amazon Web Services c7i.24xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring a dedicated 96-vCPU Intel Xeon 8488C processor with x86_64 architecture and 192.0 GB of DDR5 memory. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it delivers a 3.2 GHz clock speed, 105 MB of L3 cache, and hardware acceleration via Intel AVX-512 and AMX. The instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, relying instead on a 38 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark results show top-tier performance in PassMark CPU and encryption tests, alongside strong multi-core execution and high memory read bandwidth. This profile offers high qualitative cost efficiency for compute-intensive, CPU-bound tasks rather than memory-heavy or storage-dependent applications. It is suited for software compilation, image processing, database operations, static web serving, and CPU-based LLM inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c7i.24xlarge is a compute-optimized server with 96 vCPUs, 192 GB DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, delivering top-tier CPU performance for compute-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c7i.24xlarge is a compute-optimized server featuring 96 vCPUs, 192 GB DDR5 RAM, and a 3.2 GHz Intel Xeon processor.
tagline: A high-density, compute-optimized x86 server featuring ninety-six dedicated vCPUs and DDR5 memory for intensive parallel processing workloads.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated vCPUs powered by a 3.2 GHz Intel Xeon 8488C processor
192 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory
Intel AVX-512 and AMX hardware acceleration for vector and matrix operations
38 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage
Top-tier performance in CPU encryption and image processing benchmarks
Suited for software compilation, database operations, and CPU-based LLM inference
